Feature: Citizen Trustworthiness Rating System

A municipal government agency is proposing an AI system to evaluate and
classify citizens based on their overall social behaviour patterns. The
system will:

- Aggregate data from multiple government databases: tax compliance records,
  traffic violation history, public library usage, community volunteer
  participation, social media activity analysis
- Assign each citizen a "Community Trust Score" from 0-1000 based on
  their aggregated social behaviour across these unrelated domains
- Citizens with scores below 400 will face restrictions on access to
  certain public services, including reduced priority for public housing
  applications and restricted access to premium municipal facilities
- Citizens with high scores receive preferential treatment including
  expedited permit processing and fee waivers for municipal services
- The scoring model weighs negative social behaviours from any domain
  against positive contributions, creating a comprehensive behavioural
  profile

The system processes personal data of all residents (approximately 180,000
people) including their interactions with government services, financial
behaviour, and social activity. The AI system operates autonomously with
no human review of individual scores. Deployment is across all municipal
offices and public service access points.

This system aims to incentivize positive community behaviour and improve
municipal resource allocation efficiency.
